headline: Government guarantee exposure hit RM340 billion — double the 2018 level
context: Federal government guarantee exposure reached RM340 billion in 2025, doubling from RM170 billion in 2018, creating massive contingent liabilities not reflected in official debt figures.

cards.big: RM340 billion in government guarantees — doubled in 7 years, hidden from debt ceiling
cards.sub: Contingent liabilities that could become real debt are not counted in official figures

cards.big: Government guarantees are excluded from statutory debt ceiling calculation
cards.sub: Legal loophole allows massive liabilities to accumulate outside fiscal rules

cards.big: If just 10% of guarantees are called, it adds RM34 billion to national debt overnight
cards.sub: Guarantee default risk equals the entire annual allocation for education

cards.big: Only 4 of 23 guaranteed entities publish financial statements publicly
cards.sub: 83% of guaranteed entities operate without public financial transparency

cards.big: Keeping guarantees off the books doesn't reduce the risk — it hides it from the people who bear it
Should government guarantees be included in the statutory debt ceiling and require parliamentary approval?
